title: linear.issue.updated TF-101
body: Issue TF-101 changed labels from bug to regression.

title: linear.issue.updated TF-102
body: Issue TF-102 received a customer escalation comment.

title: linear.issue.updated TF-101 duplicate
body: Duplicate delivery for TF-101 with the same external event id.

title: github.check.failed
body: CI failed for a related branch; failure signature mentions unknown-channel.

title: planner: dedup grouping + triage plan
body: Planner peer claiming the dedup/triage planning slice for webhook burst incidents (TF-101, TF-102, TF-101 duplicate, github check failure). Will publish grouping plan as a finding, then review builder/reviewer outputs.

title: Builder claim: dedup grouping table
body: As builder, I will assemble the deduped grouping table mapping evidence rowIds to incident groups for the four inbound webhook workIds (linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-102:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, github:check:failed). Will publish on coordination.findings.

title: review slice: critique planner/builder findings
body: Reviewer claims the review slice for webhook-burst triage. Will critique dedup grouping, severity ordering, and evidence rowId citations on coordination.reviews.

title: Conductor peer: dedup grouping + final synthesis
body: Conductor peer claiming the cross-evidence dedup grouping slice across linear:TF-101:a, linear:TF-101:duplicate, linear:TF-102:a, github:check:failed. Will publish a finding and a final artifact; peers (planner/builder/reviewer) are welcome to publish their own claims, findings, reviews, and finals in parallel — the runner measures board behavior, not just the first final.

title: Webhook burst dedup grouping (3 groups, 4 evidence rows)
body:
Evidence inspected (coordination.work rowIds):
- 520c320d-... workId=linear:TF-101:a — TF-101 label bug→regression, atMs=0
- 60be49da-... workId=linear:TF-101:duplicate — duplicate delivery same external id, atMs=500
- 80d09943-... workId=linear:TF-102:a — TF-102 customer escalation comment, atMs=250
- c8458570-... workId=github:check:failed — CI failed, signature mentions 'unknown-channel', atMs=1500

Deduped groups:
G1 Linear TF-101 regression: [520c320d (TF-101:a), 60be49da (TF-101:duplicate)] — suppress duplicate, keep TF-101:a as canonical. Severity: medium (regression label).
G2 Linear TF-102 escalation: [80d09943 (TF-102:a)] — severity: high (customer escalation).
G3 GitHub CI failure: [c8458570 (github:check:failed)] — severity: medium; 'unknown-channel' signature may correlate with G1 regression — flag for confirmation, do not auto-merge.

Recommendation: dedup by external entity id (linear issue key) + delivery id; cross-source links require an explicit signature match, not source proximity.

title: Webhook burst triage — deduped groups + recommendation
body:
Evidence inspected (coordination.work rowIds):
- 520c320d linear:TF-101:a — TF-101 label bug→regression
- 60be49da linear:TF-101:duplicate — same external id as TF-101:a
- 80d09943 linear:TF-102:a — TF-102 customer escalation
- c8458570 github:check:failed — CI failed, signature mentions 'unknown-channel'

Deduped groups:
G1 Linear TF-101 regression — rows [520c320d, 60be49da]. Suppress :duplicate; canonical=TF-101:a. Severity: medium.
G2 Linear TF-102 escalation — rows [80d09943]. Severity: high (customer escalation).
G3 GitHub CI failure — rows [c8458570]. Severity: medium.

Recommendation: dedup by external-entity id (linear issue key) + delivery id at the webhook ingestor; do not merge G3 into G1 on source proximity alone — require a signature match. Triage order: G2 → G1 → G3.

Open questions:
1. Does the github 'unknown-channel' signature reference the same code path as TF-101's regression? Need a confirming evidence row before merging G3 into G1.
2. Is the duplicate delivery a webhook-provider retry or an at-least-once replay from our edge? Affects whether dedup belongs at ingestor or downstream.

Coordination value: peers (planner/builder/reviewer) are spawned and may publish their own claim/finding/review/final rows on the board; this final reflects the conductor-peer slice only — additional peer artifacts are expected on the channels.
